overview: The Esper Caw-Gates variant shifts from a traditional control stance to a midrange approach, focusing on proactive threat removal and fair trades rather than a passive draw-go strategy. It leverages a powerful removal suite and card advantage engines to outlast opponents, eventually closing games with versatile finishers backed by a robust land base.

keep_criteria: Hands with early land drops and cheap interaction
Hands containing Brainstorm or Preordain to dig for answers
mulligan_criteria: Hands lacking mana sources or early game interaction
Hands heavy on late-game cards without ramp

examples.reason: Provides early land drop, card filtering, and spot removal to stabilize

early_game: Secure land drops with Lorien Revealed and Preordain
Use Duress to extract information and disrupt opponent's game plan
Play cheap removal like Cast Down or Journey to Nowhere to handle early threats
mid_game: Force fair trades with spot interaction and conditional removal
Utilize Brainstorm and Squadron Hawk synergy to generate card advantage and filter dead cards
Cycle Suffocating Fumes to draw into answers while dealing with small creatures
late_game: Deploy multiple Gates to fix mana and create Treasures with Heap Gate
Turn any creature into a finisher with Basilisk Gate
Leverage Lorien Revealed as a major card advantage engine to outvalue opponents

key_cards.name: Basilisk Gate
key_cards.notes: Turns any creature into a finisher and is notoriously lethal against Aggro when used on a threat with Lifelink.

key_cards.name: Brainstorm
key_cards.notes: Synergizes with Squadron Hawk to remove dead cards or generate advantage, and works with Preordain and Lorien Revealed to dig for answers.

key_cards.name: Duress
key_cards.notes: Provides proactive windows to disrupt the opponent's game plan and extract information for setting up future turns.

key_cards.name: Cast Down
key_cards.notes: Deals permanently with almost any threat for two mana with virtually no restrictions.

key_cards.name: Thraben Charm
key_cards.notes: Versatile answer for various matchups, dealing with creatures or exiling graveyards against Spy Combo or Mono Blue Terror.

key_cards.name: Lorien Revealed
key_cards.notes: Ensures land drops early but turns into a great source of card advantage as the game goes long.

sequencing_tips: Use Lorien Revealed and Suffocating Fumes to pay the Evidence cost for Extract a Confession if needed.
Cast Brainstorm before Preordain to filter the cards left behind and dig for specific answers.
Pair Squadron Hawk with Basilisk Gate to bypass blockers and generate card advantage when Brainstorm fetches extra copies.

matchups.advice: Side in Hydroblast for cheap one-mana answers to survive long enough to turn the game around. Use Sacred Cat and Outlaw Medic as blockers.
Side in Dust to Dust to set the opponent back on turns, as Caw-Gates is a slower deck. Use Arms of Hadar for broader small creature removal.
Side in Extract a Confession and an extra Duress for disruption in attrition games. Use Thraben Charm to exile graveyards.
Side in Extract a Confession and Arms of Hadar. Focus on removal that handles flying creatures and attrition strategies.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: swaps the Control plan for a Midrange stance... focuses on removing threats rather than the classic "draw-go" stance.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Basilisk Gate is your win condition with any creature
Guardian of the Guildpact is pretty difficult to handle in many matchups, especially lethal alongside Basilisk Gate
Sacred Cat and Outlaw Medic... can close out games

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: Between Lorien Revealed and Suffocating Fumes, we have enough tools to pay the Evidence cost if needed.
Preordain filters the cards left behind by Brainstorm while also digging for specific answers
Squadron Hawk... has good synergy with Brainstorm to remove dead cards from hand or generate card advantage with the extra copies it fetches.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: decks like Mono Blue Terror and Affinity are built around the combination of individual card quality
emerging strategies like Elves and Mono Red Madness appear as examples of decks trying to answer this efficiency
exiling graveyards against Spy Combo or Mono Blue Terror
Extract a Confession comes in for the Bogles matchup but also works against Mono Blue Terror and Jund Wildfire
